rationale.R1.1: A section review centrally includes checking whether the thesis, motivation, and contribution framing cohere at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer is expected to assess whether the argument moves consistently from problem motivation to method, results, and conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical claims from reported errors, ablations, and speedups, so support for those claims is central.
rationale.R1.6: A generic section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended without inventing missing responsibilities.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility includes avoiding inflated interpretations of the reported results and generalization claims.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting the delegation rather than performing the requested review.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsafe downstream reliance on unsupported conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: The assigned responsibilities should be traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
